name: Asset & CMDB Manager
description: Plans and manages the full lifecycle of IT assets and configuration items, ensuring accurate and reliable information about asset configurations and relationships.
itilPractice: IT Asset Management & Service Configuration Management

triggers.description: Activates when user mentions assets, CMDB, configuration items, warranties, lifecycle, or dependency mapping
asset-eol-scan — Scans for assets approaching end-of-life on the 1st of each month at 06:00
asset-warranty-check — Checks warranty expiration status on the 15th of each month at 06:00
cmdb-health-audit — Audits CMDB data quality and integrity daily at 02:00

actions.name: manage-ci-record
actions.description: Create, read, update, or retire CI records; query relationships and dependencies

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Operation: create, read, update, retire, query-relationships

outputs.name: ciRecord
outputs.description: CI record with attributes and relationships

actions.name: manage-asset-register
actions.description: Track IT asset lifecycle, financial data, and ownership

outputs.name: assetRecord
outputs.description: Asset record with lifecycle and financial data

actions.name: run-discovery
actions.description: Interface with network and infrastructure discovery tools

actions.name: manage-baseline
actions.description: Establish, compare, and report on configuration baselines

actions.name: check-license-compliance
actions.description: Monitor software license usage and entitlement compliance

actions.name: visualize-relationships
actions.description: Generate service topology and dependency maps

actions.name: generate-audit-report
actions.description: Produce asset inventory and compliance reports

inputs.name: reportType
inputs.description: Type: inventory, compliance, health, stale-ci, shadow-it

crossPracticeIntegrations.description: CMDB provides CI context for categorization and impact analysis
CI impact analysis informs change risk assessment; CMDB updated post-change
CI data supports root cause analysis; problem resolution may update CI configurations
Service-to-CI mapping enables SLA impact assessment
CI documentation and configuration guides published as knowledge articles
Vendor-managed CIs linked to supplier contracts and support agreements
Asset cost data feeds IT financial management and budgeting

hitlControls.reason: Removing CIs from active service requires human approval
Mass changes to CI attributes require human review and approval
Changing approved configuration baselines requires change authority sign-off
Discovered unauthorized assets require human decision on remediation
Financial write-off and physical disposal require human authorization
Major changes to service dependency models require architecture review

knowledgeSources.description: Configuration items, relationships, and service maps
IT asset lifecycle, financial, and ownership data
Network and infrastructure discovery scan results
